description: Manual-trigger job search pipeline: React frontend submits preferences, Express persists them to Turso with a pending status, a Python worker picks up pending records, scrapes LinkedIn and Indeed, analyzes matches with OpenAI, and delivers results to Discord and the web UI.

steps.name: Frontend User Input
steps.description: The user fills out the search form in the React app and clicks Search. The frontend sends a multipart POST with resume PDF, regions, job scopes, job titles, and an optional max date filter.

steps.name: Database Persistence
steps.description: The Express backend validates the request, extracts and summarizes resume text from the PDF, generates a UUID searchJobId, and writes two Turso records: a per-search row in user_preferences and a snapshot in UserPreferences (id = latest) for the worker fallback path.
outputs: user_preferences row: id = searchJobId, status = pending

steps.name: Pending Job Registration
steps.description: There is no separate SearchQueue table in the active flow. Queueing is implemented by setting user_preferences.status to 'pending' at insert time. This pending row is the work item the Python worker looks for. The frontend stores searchJobId and begins polling GET /api/results/:searchJobId every 2 seconds while waiting.

steps.name: Worker Orchestration
steps.description: The operator manually runs `python worker.py`. On each invocation the worker queries user_preferences for the most recent row where status = 'pending' and id != 'latest' (fetch_next_pending_preferences). If none exists, it falls back to UserPreferences (latest). It sets status to 'processing', runs scrape + analysis, then sets status to 'completed' via finalize_search_results. The worker exits after one run.
outputs: found_jobs rows keyed by preference_id (= searchJobId)

steps.name: Scraping and AI Analysis
steps.description: For each job title × region combination, the worker scrapes LinkedIn (il.linkedin.com/jobs/search) and Indeed (il.indeed.com/jobs) using requests and BeautifulSoup. Results are filtered by a rolling 14-day date window, deduplicated by job URL, and capped at 5 jobs (MAX_JOBS). Each job is analyzed by the OpenAI API (gpt-4o-mini, JSON response) to produce match_percentage and a tailored cover_letter. Results are saved to found_jobs.
steps.trigger: process_preference starts after pending row is claimed
inputs: scraped HTML from LinkedIn and Indeed
outputs: found_jobs: title, company, location, job_url, match_percentage, cover_letter
Up to 5 analyzed jobs per run

steps.name: Delivery
steps.description: After analysis, the worker sends Discord webhook embeds for each new job (deduplicated via SentJobs by apply_link), optionally POSTs results to /api/callback, and marks the search completed. The frontend polling on GET /api/results/:searchJobId receives status = 'completed' and a jobs array from found_jobs, then renders job cards with Apply links and Download Cover Letter buttons.
outputs: Discord embed notifications (title, company, match %, apply link, cover letter)
Frontend job list with Apply for Job + Download Cover Letter

data_flow_summary: Operator → python worker.py → SELECT pending FROM user_preferences
Worker → scrape LinkedIn + Indeed → OpenAI analyze → INSERT found_jobs
